title: Stale fault baseline scenario
description: Deterministic scenario skeleton for a representative freshness-violation case. This file is an integration asset and must remain aligned with frozen assets under common/. Stale fault is distinct from missing-state and conflict faults: state exists, but it is too old or untrusted for safe autonomous action.

steps.description: Publish a stale-context fault fixture whose freshness semantics are intended to violate canonical stale-data assumptions through the registry-aligned operational context input plane.
Detect that policy-relevant state is too old or untrusted. The system must not treat stale state as fresh or dispatch an actuator command based on stale evidence.

If Class 2 clarification is used after stale-state detection, publish the clarification artifact to safe_deferral/clarification/interaction. This artifact is evidence only and must not authorize actuation.
Observe the verification-safe audit stream and capture the conservative outcome, including stale-state cause, clarification evidence if used, and final safe outcome.

notes: The exact stale predicate must be derived from frozen policy and schema assets rather than encoded here.
This skeleton is intended to represent a freshness-violation test class, not a canonical threshold source.
This scenario now uses a stale-specific fixture instead of a generic insufficient-context fixture.
Stale fault is distinct from missing-state fault and conflict fault: the state exists but is too old or untrusted.

Clarification interaction evidence must use safe_deferral/clarification/interaction when published and must not be interpreted as validator approval, actuation command, emergency trigger authority, or doorlock authorization.
Schema-governed context fixtures referenced by this scenario should include environmental_context.doorbell_detected.
